pool.baseline: it’s just so quiet here. i walk into the kitchen and i still expect to hear him making coffee or something. everything feels so pointless now that he's gone. i feel like i'm just walking around a ghost house.

pool.baseline: i was just looking at some photos of deep space and it hit me... like, everything we do, every single thing, is just a tiny blip in a universe that doesn't care. in a few billion years the sun dies, then eventually everything just goes cold and dark forever. it makes everything feel so pointless. why do we even bother trying to survive if the end result is just nothingness?

pool.baseline: i dont even know why i'm still waking up for this. the lab results came back today and everything is just... gone. 4 years of work, the funding is being pulled, and i feel like there's nothing left of me. i'm just so incredibly tired of fighting. i feel like i'm hitting a wall that i can't move past no matter how hard i try.

pool.baseline: The candlelight flickers low, much like the embers of my own soul. To be betrayed by the one heart I deemed my sanctuary... it is a cruelty even the heavens would find excessive. I feel as though I am nothing more than a ghost haunting the halls of my own life, merely waiting for the darkness to finally claim what little remains. Is there truly any grace left for one so broken, or is the only mercy found in the quiet of the shroud?
